feed.title: Munder Difflin Blog
feed.subtitle: Guides, deep dives, and comparisons on running multi-agent Claude Code: orchestration, agent memory, automation, and the tooling landscape.

entry.title: How to Install and Use Munder Difflin
entry.summary: Install Munder Difflin on macOS, Windows, or Linux and put a hive of Claude Code agents to work on ambitious, long-horizon tasks — start to finish.

entry.title: Running AI Agents Safely: Permission Modes and Sandboxing
entry.summary: How to run autonomous AI agents safely: permission modes and the bypassPermissions foot-gun, workspace isolation, and what to gate vs. allow.

entry.title: Munder Difflin's Architecture: Two Data Planes, One Renderer
entry.summary: A walkthrough of the multi-agent harness architecture: a node-pty terminal plane and a hooks/hive event plane feeding one React + Pixi.js renderer.

entry.title: The Best Tools to Run Multiple Claude Code Agents (2026)
entry.summary: An honest 2026 roundup of tools to run multiple Claude Code agents — Claude Squad, Conductor, Crystal, vibe-kanban, and Munder Difflin — compared.

entry.title: Building Reliable AI Agents: Failure Is the Default
entry.summary: Agents fail constantly: tools error, steps stall, output goes wrong. Reliability isn't avoiding failure — it's making each one contained and recoverable.

entry.title: Can Claude Code Agents Talk to Each Other?
entry.summary: Can Claude Code agents talk to each other? By default they report to their launcher — but a small coordination layer lets them message peer-to-peer.

entry.title: Claude Code Automation: Let Agents Build While You Sleep
entry.summary: Claude Code automation that runs overnight: an autonomous Stop-hook loop, safe permission bypass, and guardrails so you wake up to progress, not chaos.

entry.title: Git Worktrees vs a Hive: Two Ways to Parallelize Claude Code
entry.summary: Claude Code git worktrees vs a hive: when isolated worktrees suffice for parallel agents, and when you need memory, messaging, and an orchestrator on top.

entry.title: Claude Code Orchestration Tools, Compared
entry.summary: Claude Code orchestration tools compared on memory, messaging, visibility, control, and local-first — across Claude Squad, Conductor, Crystal, and more.

entry.title: Claude Squad vs Munder Difflin: Which Should You Use?
entry.summary: Claude Squad vs Munder Difflin: a lean terminal session manager against a memory-backed, orchestrated, visual hive. Feature table and an honest verdict.

entry.title: Context Engineering for AI Agents: Budgeting the Window
entry.summary: Why context engineering beats prompt wording for agents — and the tactics (isolation, retrieval, externalized state, compaction) that keep the window lean.

entry.title: How to Debug a Multi-Agent System
entry.summary: How to debug a multi-agent system: use the event log, per-agent terminals, message trails, and git history to find why a hive of AI agents went sideways.

entry.title: Do More With Less: Smart Model Routing Across Agents
entry.summary: Why a hive of agents shouldn't all run the biggest model — and how routing the right task to the right model cuts cost and latency without losing quality.

entry.title: How AI Agents Remember: Semantic Memory in a Hive
entry.summary: A code-grounded guide to how AI agents remember — the MemPalace mine loop, per-agent wings, and wake-up digest that give a Claude Code hive shared recall.

entry.title: How AI Agents Verify Their Own Work (Before Saying 'Done')
entry.summary: The discipline that makes an autonomous coding agent trustworthy: prove every claim, reproduce the green, and check the fix — not just the intent.

entry.title: Approving AI Agents: Human-in-the-Loop Without a Queue
entry.summary: How to approve what AI agents do without a parallel approval queue: native permission prompts, routing 'to: human' to the orchestrator, remote approval.

entry.title: Keep Your Agent's Semantic Memory Clean: Don't Index the Config
entry.summary: A semantic memory layer is only as good as what you feed it — how an agent's recall got swamped by config and logs, and the .gitignore + prune fix.

entry.title: Local-First AI Agent Orchestration: How a Hive Runs Without a Cloud
entry.summary: How local-first AI agent orchestration works under the hood — the loop, mailboxes, scheduler, and audit log that coordinate a hive on one machine.

entry.title: Building AI Dev Tools in the Open, On Purpose
entry.summary: Agents touch your code, keys, and memory. That's why agent tooling should be open source and local-first — so you can verify it, not just trust it.

entry.title: Overnight AI Coding: What's Realistic (and What's Not)
entry.summary: An honest look at letting AI coding agents run overnight — what works today, where guardrails matter, and what still needs a human in the morning.

entry.title: Prompt Caching for AI Agents: Stop Paying for the Same Tokens Twice
entry.summary: An agent re-sends the same prompt, tools, and context every turn. Prompt caching makes you pay for that prefix once — here's how to design for it.

entry.title: Make a Hive Work While You Sleep: Scheduling Autonomous Agent Missions
entry.summary: How scheduled missions make a Claude Code hive work on a cadence — recurring requests that fire on an interval and land in an agent's queue, hands-free.

entry.title: Security for AI Coding Agents: A Practical Guide
entry.summary: How to run AI coding agents safely: contain the blast radius, scope every task, treat agent input as untrusted, and gate the irreversible behind a human.

entry.title: A Small-Team Playbook for AI Coding Agents
entry.summary: How a small team adopts a hive of AI coding agents without chaos — the roles to assign, the cadence to run, and the guardrails that keep it safe.

entry.title: The Office Parody Behind Munder Difflin (and Why It Helps)
entry.summary: Why Munder Difflin is a loving parody of The Office — and how the office metaphor makes a hive of AI agents genuinely easier to understand and trust.

entry.title: Trigger Your AI Agent Hive from Slack
entry.summary: Trigger an AI agent hive from Slack: a local webhook verifies each message and drops it into your orchestrator's queue as a task — no server to host.

entry.title: AEO for Dev Tools: Getting Cited by ChatGPT and Claude
entry.summary: Answer Engine Optimization for dev tools: how to get cited by ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity with the right robots.txt, JSON-LD, and writing patterns.

entry.title: What's New in Agentic AI: A June 2026 Field Guide
entry.summary: A grounded June 2026 roundup of agentic AI: Claude Opus 4.8, the MCP and A2A protocol layer, usage-based agent billing, and agent governance.

entry.title: Building a Terminal UI with xterm.js and node-pty
entry.summary: Wire xterm.js to a node-pty backend: stream output, write keystrokes back, handle resize, and keep many live terminals fast with a terminal pool.

entry.title: Building an AI "Office Floor": Avatars, Pathfinding, Envelopes
entry.summary: The game-dev techniques behind a dev tool's office floor: Tiled maps, BFS pathfinding, sprite recoloring, and flying message envelopes in Pixi.js.

entry.title: Claude Code Hooks, Explained (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop)
entry.summary: A practical tour of Claude Code hooks — the PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop lifecycle — and how a Unix-socket hook shim drives a live office floor.

entry.title: Looking for a Claude Squad Alternative?
entry.summary: What Claude Squad does well, where it stops, and why a memory-backed, orchestrated hive may be the Claude Squad alternative you're after.

entry.title: A Conductor Alternative for Orchestrating Claude Code
entry.summary: Conductor is a polished macOS app for parallel Claude Code worktrees. When to pick a cross-platform, orchestrated hive as your Conductor alternative.

entry.title: A Crystal Alternative: Multi-Agent Claude Code with Memory
entry.summary: Crystal runs parallel Claude Code worktrees well. Where a memory-backed, orchestrated hive changes the workflow — and when to stick with Crystal.

entry.title: Bringing Your MCP Servers and Skills Into a Hive of Agents
entry.summary: How agents in a hive inherit your existing Claude Code MCP servers, skills, and tools — and how to scope which agent gets which capability.

entry.title: Real Terminals in Electron with node-pty
entry.summary: How to run byte-for-byte authentic shells inside Electron with node-pty: the native-rebuild gotcha, the macOS PATH trap, and streaming PTY output over IPC.

entry.title: Rendering Many Live Terminals Without Melting the CPU
entry.summary: xterm.js performance for many live PTYs: a terminal pool, render-only-visible, smart scrollback, and accelerated rendering to stream dozens at once.

entry.title: A vibe-kanban Alternative for Coordinating AI Agents
entry.summary: vibe-kanban gives you a board to assign tasks to coding agents. When a self-routing hive is the better vibe-kanban alternative — and when a board wins.

entry.title: The AI Coding Agent Glossary: Harness, Orchestrator, Hive, Subagent
entry.summary: Plain-English definitions of the AI coding agent terms everyone trips over — harness, orchestrator, hive, subagent, agent memory — each in one line.

entry.title: From One Terminal to a Team: Scaling Your Claude Code Workflow
entry.summary: The workflow shift from a single Claude Code session to a coordinated team of agents — what changes, where it breaks, and the concrete before-and-after.

entry.title: How to Choose a Multi-Agent Coding Tool: A Buyer's Checklist
entry.summary: A decision framework for choosing a multi-agent coding tool — memory, control, visibility, cost, local-first — with a simple scoring rubric you can run.

entry.title: Human-in-the-Loop AI Agents: Approvals That Don't Slow You Down
entry.summary: Design human-in-the-loop AI agents that stay autonomous: an approvals queue that escalates only spend, destructive ops, and scope changes.

entry.title: Munder Difflin FAQ: Everything People Ask
entry.summary: Answers to the top Munder Difflin questions — what it is, is it free, does it run locally, which platforms, and how it differs from many terminals.

entry.title: Run an Office of AI Agents While You Sleep
entry.summary: What it takes to run a self-coordinating office of AI coding agents that keeps shipping after you log off — and the guardrails that keep it sane.

entry.title: The Single-Committer Pattern: Multi-Agent Git Without Corruption
entry.summary: Parallel agents corrupt a repo with index.lock races. The single-committer pattern — agents write, one process commits — fixes concurrent git writes.

entry.title: Visualizing AI Agents with Pixi.js: An Office You Can Watch
entry.summary: How we render AI agents as avatars on a Pixi.js office floor — driven by real hook and message events, with seat assignment, pathing, and flying envelopes.

entry.title: What Are Claude Code Agents? And How to Use Many at Once
entry.summary: Claude Code agents explained in plain English — what an agent actually is, how subagents differ, and the leap from one agent to a coordinated team.

entry.title: Why Local-First Matters for Your AI Agents
entry.summary: The control, privacy, and cost case for keeping your AI agents and their memory on your own machine — and what cloud orchestration quietly costs.

entry.title: Why We Built Munder Difflin
entry.summary: The origin story of Munder Difflin — how the pain of juggling Claude Code terminals led to a coordinated, memory-backed hive of agents you can watch.

entry.title: An Append-Only Event Log for a Hive of Agents
entry.summary: How an append-only event log makes a multi-agent system debuggable and replayable: what to record, and why one JSON line per event beats a database.

entry.title: How to Run Multiple Claude Code Agents (Without Losing Track)
entry.summary: Run several Claude Code agents in parallel without the chaos: give each a role, let them coordinate, and stop alt-tabbing between terminal windows.

entry.title: Atomic File Mailboxes: How Agents Message Each Other
entry.summary: Can Claude Code agents talk to each other? Yes — the outbox-router-inbox design that lets agents message safely using plain files and atomic renames.

entry.title: Inside the GOD Orchestrator: Routing, Adjudication, Escalation
entry.summary: A deep dive into the Claude Code orchestrator: how a GOD agent reads requests, routes work, adjudicates routine traffic, and escalates the critical few.

entry.title: The Best Way to Coordinate AI Coding Agents
entry.summary: The best way to coordinate AI coding agents: single-writer files, a message router, a one-scribe plan, and an orchestrator — cooperate, not clobber.

entry.title: Orchestrating Claude Code Agents: A Practical Guide
entry.summary: How to orchestrate Claude Code agents in practice: what orchestration means, how a GOD orchestrator routes and escalates work, and how to wire it up.

entry.title: How to Give Claude Code Long-Term Memory
entry.summary: Claude Code forgets between sessions. Here's how a markdown-first memory layer with semantic recall lets your agents remember across runs.

entry.title: Why We Built Agent Memory Markdown-First
entry.summary: Markdown-first AI agent long-term memory: plain notes a human can read and git can diff, with a semantic index on top that degrades gracefully.

entry.title: Semantic Memory for AI Agents: Recall in Milliseconds
entry.summary: What semantic memory for AI agents is, why a markdown-first store beats a heavy DB, and how a shared palace lets a hive recall a note by meaning, fast.

entry.title: How to Manage Multiple Claude Code Sessions Like a Pro
entry.summary: Tactics to manage multiple Claude Code sessions without losing track: naming, roles, context isolation, and when a harness beats juggling terminal tabs.

entry.title: Set Up a Claude Code Multi-Agent Workflow in 10 Minutes
entry.summary: A from-zero Claude Code multi-agent setup: install Munder Difflin, onboard, spawn a few agents, and watch the GOD orchestrator route your first task.
